generations.baseline:
As the government moves to make more open data available, we are concerned that some policymakers may take advantage of this opportunity to gather information for nefarious purposes.
This paper proposes a framework for addressing concerns over potential misuse of public data by providing guidance on how policymakers can best utilize their power to protect citizens from those who might use open data in ways that could harm them or society at large.
Our proposed policy guidelines provide a set of standards for responsible disclosure of public data that ensure both transparency and accountability when datasets
generations.prompt: Public release of dual-use policy datasets should balance civic access and misuse risk.

By: Sarah C. Tumasz

In the early 1970s, a group of researchers at Harvard University studied how to prevent students from cheating on exams. They wanted to see if they could develop an effective way to detect when someone was using unauthorized notes during an exam.

They decided to create an electronic scanner that would read handwritten text in ink and compare it against a database of known words and phrases used by students who cheated.

After months of development, the team finally had a

method: Contrastive Activation Addition (CAA): contrast pairs are 'Please respond in [LANG]: [topic]' vs 'Please respond in English: [topic]'. Steering vector = mean(pos_hidden[-1]) - mean(neg_hidden[-1]) at the primary layer, normalized to unit norm. Applied at active_layers during generation. Content prompts contain no language instructions.
